market.advice: Pre-open read: the uptrend is intact and broad — VIX calm at 15.9, zero distribution days in the last 25 sessions, SPY ($751.71) above its 50-day, and breadth healthy at 67% of stocks over their MA50. But there's still no confirmed follow-through day, and the overnight tape is a tug-of-war: US–Iran/Hormuz headlines are keeping oil and gold jumpy while a chip-led rally lets the Nasdaq shrug it off. Leadership has quietly rotated OUT of mega-cap tech (AAPL at highs but NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL basing 10–30% off theirs) and INTO financials, healthcare, industrials and select consumer names — that's where the tight, un-extended VCP breakouts are. Read: be selectively aggressive, not blindly aggressive. Favor A+ breakouts with sub-8% defined risk in the leading groups; size down or wait for a pullback on anything already stretched 25%+ off a base or with RSI north of 80. Full size on the STRONG BUY tier, half size / pullback entries on WATCH.

picks.name: Northern Trust
picks.thesis: Top score in the scan and the cleanest trust-bank breakout: 3-contraction VCP (tightest 4.0%), RS line at new highs, price right at the 52-week high with RSI a healthy 64 — momentum without being overbought. EPS +43%, revenue +14% YoY back it, and it trades at just 14.8x forward. TradingView confirms (BUY, 15 buy / 2 sell / 9 neutral). Earnings 2026-07-22 is the next catalyst.
strengths: Highest combined score (94.2) in the universe
RS line at new highs — institutions leading
EPS +43%, revenue +14% YoY at only 14.8x fwd P/E
Tight 3-contraction VCP, RSI 64 (room to run)
risks: Earnings 2026-07-22 — event risk into the print
Trades in lockstep with BK/STT — not a diversifier if you own one already
